title: SMPTE Opens Standards Library for Free Access
summary: The Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers (SMPTE) has announced that its entire library of media technology standards is now freely accessible to the public, removing previous paywalls. This move removes a major barrier to innovation in media production and distribution, enabling broader adoption and collaboration on open standards across the industry. The free access includes standards such as SMPTE timecode and VC-1, and is part of SMPTE's modernization efforts including GitHub workflows and structured HTML authoring.
context: SMPTE is a professional society that develops technical standards for the motion picture and television industries, including the widely-used SMPTE timecode. Previously, access to these standards required payment, which limited adoption and innovation.
discussion: Comments largely welcomed the move, with lambdaone praising it as aiding the shift to open standards like the IETF, while geerlingguy questioned why any standards body wouldn't do this by default. ksec noted the modernization initiatives like GitHub adoption.

references.title: Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers - Wikipedia
